# k8s-lab

> Reusable building blocks for a single-host Kubernetes laboratory on
> Debian or Ubuntu Linux. LXC/LXD system containers are the Kubernetes nodes;
> the Cluster API provider for Incus/LXD (CAPN) drives the cluster lifecycle;
> every Kubernetes object is delivered as a Helm chart through Terraform.
> A Molecule + Vagrant + libvirt harness exercises the same code path on a
> developer laptop as on production hardware.

The repository ships only reusable building blocks (Ansible roles, a Terraform
module, Helm charts, a test harness). Site-specific composition lives in
separate private consumer repositories that import this code. Stage 1 is
closed as v1.0.

## Documentation

- [Overview](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/01-overview.md): Core idea, scope, goals, non-goals.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/02-architecture.md): Single-host model, dual-NIC node design, canonical bootstrap → pivot → workload flow, layer ownership between Ansible, Terraform, and Helm.
- [Stack](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/03-stack.md): Every external dependency and its pinned version (Kubernetes v1.35, k3s v1.35.3, Cluster API v1.12.5, CAPN v0.8.5, LXD snap 6/stable, Calico v3.31.5, MetalLB v0.15.3, Terraform hashicorp/helm 3.1.1).
- [Repository structure](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/04-repository-structure.md): Directory layout, naming rules, variable conventions, role / chart / module boundaries.
- [Prerequisites](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/05-prerequisites.md): Host requirements, runner setup, network plan.
- [Quickstart local](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/06-quickstart-local.md): Vagrant + libvirt VM, full end-to-end in one make target.
- [Deployment guide](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/07-deployment-guide.md): Real-host deployment via a private consumer repository.
- [Configuration reference](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/08-configuration-reference.md): Project globals, role inputs, Terraform inputs/outputs, chart values.
- [Roles reference](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/09-roles-reference.md): All 14 Ansible roles in one place.
- [Modules and charts](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/10-modules-and-charts.md): The Terraform module and 5 Helm charts.
- [Operations](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/11-operations.md): Day-2 — workloads, kubeconfigs, destroy chain, upgrades.
- [Testing](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/12-testing.md): Molecule + Vagrant + libvirt harness, scenario set, gate checks.
- [Troubleshooting](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/13-troubleshooting.md): Common failures and recipes.
- [Glossary](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/doc/14-glossary.md): Acronyms and terms.

## Plans

- [Plan index](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/tree/main/plans): Continuous §N section numbering across all plan files. Source of truth for *why* each decision was made.

## Bundled snapshot

- [llms-full.txt](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Concatenation of every doc chapter in reading order, regenerated by `make docs-llm`. Use this if you want the whole user-facing documentation in a single fetch.

## Repository

- [Source](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab): The repository itself.
- [README](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/README.md): Top-level summary, badges, layout, conventions.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Scope, test policy, style.
- [Security](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Reporting policy and out-of-scope cases.
- [License](https://github.com/kogeler/k8s-lab/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT.
